titleStmt.title: SII 3.70: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
VI.—INSCRIPTIONS OF KULOTTUNGA-CHOLA I. No. 70.—INSCRIPTION AT SRIRANGAM.

summary.p: This inscription (No. 62 of 1892) is engraved on the east wall of the third prākāra of the Raṅganātha templeRegarding a few other inscriptions in the same temple see Ind. Ant. Vol. XXI. p. 344; Ep. Ind. Vol. III. pp. 7 and 117, and Vol. IV. p. 148. on the island of Śrīraṅgam near Trichinopoly. It mentions Śrīraṅgam as Tiruvaraṅgam (l. 16) and the temple as Tiruvaraṅgadēvar (l. 10).

The date is the 18th year of the reign of Kulōttuṅga I. The historical introduction does not add any fresh details to those narrated at the beginning of the inscriptions of the 14th and 15th yearsSee below, p. 151, note 1. . The inscription records that a certain Kāliṅgarāyar granted to the temple 61/4 kāśu with the condition that the interest should be applied to defraying the cost of offerings on two festival days.

As discovered by Mr. Venkayya,See above, Vol. II. p. 252, note 7. the subjoined inscription fixes the time before which two of the twelve Vaishṇava Āḻvārs, who were the authors of the Nālāyiraprabandham, must have lived. For, (1) it refers to the recital of the text beginning with Tēṭṭarundiṟal (l. 13), which is the 2nd chapter of the sacred hymns of Kulaśēkhara; and (2) the names of three of the temple officials who are mentioned in the inscription prove that the Vaishṇava saint Śaṭhagōpa or Nammāḻvār was already at that time well known and highly venerated.See below, p. 151, notes 3, 5 and 6. As noticed before, his work, the Tiruvāymoḻi, is presupposed already in an inscription of Rājarāja I.See page 2 above. These epigraphical evidences are fatal to the theory of Dr. Caldwell, who placed the Āḻvārs in the 12th or 13th century.Comparative Grammar, p. 143 of the Introduction.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . pukaḻ cūḻn=ta puṇari Akaḻ cūḻnta puviyil poṉnemiyaḷavun=tan nemi naṭappa viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai Iḷaṅkopparuvattu cakakarakoṭṭattu vikkiramattoḻalāl putumaṇam puṇarntu matutavaraiyīṭṭam vayirākarattu vāri Ayirmuṉaikkontaḷavaracar tan taḷamiriya vāḷuṟai kaḻittuttoḷ vali kāṭṭipporppari naṭāttikkikīrttiyai
niṟutti vaṭaticai vākai cūṭitteṉṟicaittemarukamalappūmakaḷ potumaiyum ponniyāṭai naṉnilappāvai tanimaiyuntavira vantu punṉitaṟttirumaṇimakuṭam Urimaiyiṟcūṭittaṉnaṭi Iraṇṭum taṭamuṭiyākattonnilaventar cūṭa munṉai manuvāṟu peruka kaliyā
ṟu vaṟuppacceṅkol ticaitoṟuñcella veṇkuṭai Irunilavaḷākam Eṅkaṇun=tanātu tiruniḻal veṇṇilāttikaḻa Orutani meruviṟpuli viḷaiyāṭa vārkaṭaṟṟiīvān=tarattuppūpālar tiṟai viṭutan=ta kalañcori kaḷiṟu muṟai niṟpa vilaṅkiya tenanavan karun=talai parun=talai
ttiṭattan ponnakar puṟattiṭaikkiṭappa Innāḷ piṟkulappiṟai pol niṟpiḻaiyennuñcolletir koṭiṟṟallatu tan kai villetir koṭā vikkalan tānaṅkili tuṭaṅki maṇalūr naṭuvenattuṅkapattiraiyaḷavunturatti veEṅkaṇum
paṭṭa veṅkaḷiṟum viṭṭa tan mānamuṅkūṟina viīramuṅkiṭappa Eṟina malaikaḷumutuku neḷippa Iḻin=ta natikaḷum cuḻanṟuṭaintoṭa viḻunta kaṭalkaḷuntalaivirittalamarakkuṭaticaittannāḷukan=tu tānum tāṉaiyum pannāḷiṭṭa palapala mutuku payattetir māṟaiya ja
yapperuntiruvum paḻiyukan=tu kuṭutta pukaḻin celviyum vāḷāroṇkaṇ maṭan=taaiyariīṭṭamumimīḷātu viṭṭa veṅkari niraiyum kaṅkamaṇṭalamum ciṅkaṇamennum pāṇi Iraṇṭum Oruvicaikkaikkoṇṭiīṇṭiya pukaḻoṭu pāṇṭimaṇṭalamum koḷḷattiruvu
ḷḷattaṭaittu veḷḷavaruparittaraṅkaḷmum porukarikkualaṅkaḷum tan=tiravāriyum Uṭaittāy van=tu vaṭakaṭal tenkaṭal paṭarvatu polattan peruñceṉai Evippañcavar Aiyvarum poruta moykkaḷattañci veru noeḷittoṭi araṇeṉappukka kāṭaṟattu
ṭaittu nānaṭṭippaṭuttu maṟṟavartammai vanacarar tiriyum poṟṟai veñcurameṟṟikakoṟṟavijaiyastampam ticaitoṟuniṟu muttina calāpamumuttamiṭpotiyilumattaveṅkari paṭumaiyyaccayyamuṅkanniyuṅkaikkoṇ
kaṭalkuṭamalaināṭṭuḷḷa cāveṟellām tani vicumpeṟa māveṟiya tan varukaniṟrūtiṉitṟtalaivarai kurukalar kulaiyakkoṭṭāṟuṭpaṭa neṟitoṟu- nilaikaḷiṭṭaruḷittiṟai koḷ vīrasiṁhāsakan=tiriya viṭṭaruḷippoṅkoḷiyāramun=tiruppuyattarlaṅkaḷulum poliya vīramun=tiyākamum viḷaṅkappārmicai mevalar vaṇaṅka vīṟṟirun=taruḷiya
kovirājakesaripanmarāna cakravattikaḷ śrīkolottuṅkacoḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉeṭṭāvatu . Āḻvār tiruvaraṅkatevarkku śrīkāriyañceykiṟa Atikārikaḷ niṣatarājar Ēval paṭiyum śrīvayiṣṇavavāriyam tiruveḻutināṭudāsarum vaṭamaturappiṟan=tānampiyum Irāyūrāḷinampiyum r nārāyaṇanampi
yum perumpaṟṟappuliyūr śrīcaṭakopadāsarum mārkkamaṅkalattu Arikulavāraṇanampiyum śrīpaṇṭāravāriyam Āritan kurukaikāvalan Ārāvamutum Āritan tiruvāykkulamuṭaiyān ciīrākavanum Āritan kecuvan taniiḷañciṅkamum pāratāyan kecuvan Aṟivariyānum pāratāyan tiruvaraṅkanārāyaṇa
n śrīkṛṣṇanum pāritan Ārāvamutu ciīrāmanum sabhaikkaṇakku Arayan Ampala ṟṟuvappiriyanum śrīvayiṣṇavakkaṇakku tiruveṅkaṭavan comanāna patineṭṭunāṭukiḻavanum Uḷḷiṭṭa Āḻv ār kanṉmikaḷom Arayan garuḍavāhanāna kāliṅkarāyarkku nāṅkaḷ sammatittuttiīṭṭukkuṭutta paricāvatu .
Appikai terttirunāḷilum paṅkūnittirunāḷilum tīrttam pirasātittaruḷina Anṟirā tiruppunnaikkiīḻ Eḻuntaruḷi Iruntu teṭṭarun=tiṟal keṭṭaruḷum potu Iṟṟai nāḷāl tirunāḷ Onṟukku Amuti ceytaruḷum Appamutu nūṟukku veṇṭum paḻavarici patakkum paruppu mun=nāḻiyum ney munnāḻiyum caṟka
rai nūṟṟuppalamum miḷaku muḻākkum ciīrakam Uḻākkum Uppu muḻākkum vāḻaippaḻam Aimpatum teṅkāy Aiñcum Iḷaniīramutu pattuma Aṭaikkāyamutu nūṟṟirupatum kaṟpūratapyilam Oruceviṭaraiyum teriilaippaṟṟu pan=niraṇṭum kaṟpūram Iraṇṭu mañcāṭiyum Appamutukku māviṭippārkkuniīraṭṭuvār
kkum viṟakiṭuvārkkum Appamutu cuṭuvārkkum kucakkalattukkum Uḷḷiṭṭu maṟṟum veṇṭuvanavaiyiṟṟukkuñcan=tirātittaval cella Ivar Oṭukkina kācu Āṟe kāl . Ikkācu Āṟe kāluṅkoṇṭu policaiyāl Iraṇṭu tirunāḷilum Ippaṭiyey can=tirātittaval Amuti ceyvikkakkaṭavomāka
vum . Ippaṭi kal veṭṭivittukkoḷḷappe vum . Ippaṭi Icain=tu sammātattiīṭṭukkuṭuttom Āḻvār kanmikaḷom . Itu can=tirātittaval celuttuvikka kaṭapvomānṉom tiruvaraṅkattu mahāsabhaiyom . Itu śrīvayiṣṇavarakṣai ..

app.note: An erased ā seems to stand after Ari.
A few indistinct letters are here entered below the line.
This is perhaps a mistake for Āritaṉ or hāritaṉ.

div.p: (Line 10.) In the [eighteenth] year (of the reign) of king Rājakēsarivarman, alias the emperor Śrī-Kulōttuṅga-Śōḻadēva, who etc.The historical introduction of this inscription is the same as in No. 69 and in the Tanjore inscription of the 15th year (above, Vol. II. No. 58). —by order of the magistrate (adhikārin) Nisha[dha]rājar, the manager of the temple of the god Tiruvaraṅgadēvar, the following was agreed on and given in writing to Arayaṉ[Garu]ḍa[v]āhaṉ, alias Kāliṅgarāyar, by us, the Pūjāris (kaṉmi) of the god, such as (1) the members of the committeeVāriyam is probably connected with vāra, on which see Professor Kielhorn's remarks in Ep. Ind. Vol. V. p. 138, note 7. It occurs also in the Ukkal inscriptions, where I have translated it by ‘elected for’ or ‘in charge of’; see p. 2 above. of the Śrī-Vaishṇavas: Tiruveḻu[di]nāḍu-Dāsar,This person was named after the Vaishṇava saint Śaṭhagōpa, who, according to the Nālāyiraprabandham, came from Tiruvaḻudi-nāḍu. Vaḍamadurappiṟandā[ṉ]-I.e. ‘he who was born in the northern Mathurā,’ viz. Kṛishṇa. Nambi, Irāyūrā[ḷi]-Nambi, . . . . . r Nārāyaṇa-Nambi, Śrīśaḍagōpa-DāsarSee note 3 above. of Perum[ba]ṟṟappuliyūr, and Arikulavāraṇa-Nambi of Mārkkamaṅgalam; (2) the members of the committee of the temple treasury: KurugaikāvalaṉI.e. ‘the protector of Kurugai.’ The saint Śaṭhagōpa was the son of Kāri, the adhikārin of the city of Kurugai. Ārāvamudu of the Hārita (gōtra), Tiruvāykkulam-UḍaiyāṉThis title is perhaps derived from Tiruvāykkulam, one of the names of the Rājagōpāla-Perumāḷ temple at Maṇimaṅgalam; see p. 49 above, and Ep. Ind. Vol. V. p. 72. Śrīrāghavaṉ of the Hārita (gōtra), Kēśuvaṉ (Kēśava) [Ta]ni-Iḷa[ñ]jiṅgam of the Hārita (gōtra), Kēśuvaṉ Aṟivariyāṉ of the Bhāradvāja (gōtra), [Tiru]va[raṅ]ga-Nārāyaṇaṉ Śrīkṛishṇaṉ of the Bhāradvāja (gōtra), and Ārāvamudu Śrīrāmaṉ of the [H]ārita (gōtra); (3) the accountants of the assembly: Arayaṉ Ambala . . . . . and . . . . . āṟṟuvappiriyaṉ; and (4) the accountant of the Śrī-Vaishṇavas: TiruvēṅgaḍavaṉThis person was called after the god of Tirupati. Sōmaṉ, alias Padineṭṭunāḍu-Kiḻavaṉ.I.e. ‘the chief of eighteen districts.’

(L. 13.) During the car festival in (the month of) Appigai (Aippaśi) and during the festival in Paṅgūni (Paṅguṉi), on the night of that day on which the bathing-water (of the idol) is distributed, at the time when (the idol) has been placed under the sacred puṉṉai (tree) and is listening to (the recital of the hymn) Tēṭṭarundiṟal, (the following requirements have to be supplied) on this day of either festival:—For one hundred cakes to be offered (to the god) are required one padakku of old rice, three nāḻi of pulse (paruppu), three nāḻi of ghee, one hundred palam of sugar, three uḻākku of pepper, one uḻākku of cumin, three uḻākku of salt, fifty plantains, five cocoa-nuts, ten young cocoa-nuts, one hundred and twenty arecanuts, one śeviḍu and a half of camphor-oil, twelve bundles of teriThis word is not found in the dictionary. Probably betel-leaves are meant. leaves, and two mañjāḍi of camphor. (For all this), for those who pound (the rice into) flour for the cakes, for those who carry water, for those who fetch firewood, for those who fry the cakes, for pots, and for other requirements, heThis refers to Kāliṅgarāyar (l. 12). deposited six and a quarter kāśu, to continue as long as the moon and the sun.

(L. 15.) Having received these six and a quarter kāśu, we shall be bound to supply the offerings in this way at both festivals out of the interest for as long as the moon and the sun shall exist. Thus [it should be] caused to be engraved on stone. Having agreed thus, we, the Pūjāris of the god, gave a written agreement.
(L. 16.) We, the great assembly of Tiruvaraṅgam, shall be bound to continue this as long as the moon and the sun exist. This (is placed under) the protection of the Śrī-Vaishṇavas.
Digital edition of SII 3.70 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
